definition: A structured human-readable description of a computational procedure.
Executable code that realizes a procedure in a specific language and runtime.
How faithfully implementation preserves pseudocode intent while becoming testable and maintainable.
How well sequence branching loops and stopping rules match intended procedure.
Attention to boundary cases such as empty input ties invalid values no solution and nontermination.
Explicit behavior for invalid input failure warnings and exceptions.
Checks connecting expected behavior to observed program behavior.
Ability to safely revise extend debug and document a program over time.
